commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `feishu_chat` registration to use an execution-time tool factory, the shared Feishu tool-account resolver, and regression tests for contextual chat-account routing.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +97. Total +103 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current `registerFeishuChatTools` binds `feishu_chat` to `accounts[0]` during registration, so a tool built in a context with `agentAccountId` cannot change the client account. I did not run a live Feishu tenant repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The next blocker is contributor real behavior proof plus maintainer acceptance of the compatibility and credential-boundary change; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.

target.title: fix(feishu): route chat tools through contextual accounts
target.body:
﻿Related: #96657

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where `feishu_chat` could route chat and member lookup tool calls through the first configured enabled Feishu account instead of the Feishu account selected for the current tool context.

The affected tool is `feishu_chat`, including its `info`, `members`, and `member_info` actions. In a multi-account Feishu setup, those actions are account-sensitive: a chat ID, member ID, or tenant-visible membership list is only meaningful for the Feishu app and tenant that can access it. If the tool creates its Lark client from the wrong configured account, users can see misleading permission errors, missing chat/member results, or data from a different configured Feishu account than the one associated with the current agent context.

Before this change, `registerFeishuChatTools` made the account decision too early. It listed enabled Feishu accounts during registration, inspected only `accounts[0]` for `tools.chat`, and then created every chat client from that same first account. That skipped the shared Feishu tool-account resolver used by neighboring Feishu tools, so execution-time context such as `agentAccountId` could not influence which account supplied the client.

That produced two concrete multi-account failure modes:

- When multiple Feishu accounts enabled chat, a tool resolved in a context such as `agentAccountId: "b"` could still execute through account `a` because `a` happened to be the first enabled configured account.
- When the first enabled account disabled `tools.chat` but a later enabled account allowed it, `feishu_chat` could fail to register even though the overall Feishu configuration contained an account that supported the chat tool.

This is related to the account-routing problem described in #96657, but the scope here is intentionally narrower. This PR only moves `feishu_chat` onto the existing Feishu tool-account routing path; it does not claim to fix every non-inbound Feishu binding, cron, relay, heartbeat, or broader tool-routing case covered by that issue.

## Why This Change Was Made

`feishu_chat` now follows the same account-selection pattern as the neighboring Feishu tools. Registration checks whether any enabled and configured account exposes chat tools, and each execution creates the client through the shared Feishu tool-account router using the current tool context.

The chat actions and Feishu API calls are unchanged. This only changes which configured Feishu account supplies the client and enforces the existing per-account `tools.chat` check for the selected account.

## User Impact

Multi-account Feishu users can run chat and member lookup tools from the contextual account instead of accidentally routing through the first configured account. Deployments where chat is enabled only on a non-first account can also expose `feishu_chat` consistently.

Single-account configurations keep the same behavior.

## Evidence

Focused regression coverage now verifies:

- `feishu_chat` routes through the contextual account when multiple accounts enable chat.
- `feishu_chat` still registers when the first account disables chat and a later account enables it.
- a disabled contextual chat account returns the existing disabled-tool error instead of silently falling back to another account.

Call chain covered by the regression:

```text
tool context with agentAccountId "b"
  -> feishu_chat action info
  -> registerFeishuChatTools
  -> createFeishuToolClient(...)
  -> selected Feishu account supplies the Lark client
```

Validation:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/feishu/src/chat.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/tool-account-routing.test.ts` - passed, 2 files / 30 tests
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test-local-feishu-chat.tsbuildinfo` - passed
- `pnpm test:extension feishu` - passed, 73 files / 972 tests
- `git diff --check` - passed

AI-assisted: yes, prepared with Codex GPT-5.5 xhigh review assistance.
